raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need find files older than 7 days and compress in parallel. Use find with -mtime +7 and -type f, then xargs -P? Use pigz for parallel gzip. Command: find testbed -type f -mtime +7 -print0 | xargs -0 -P$(nproc) pigz -k? But compress in place? pigz -k keeps original. Use -p? Actually pigz -p threads. But parallel: xargs -P. So: find testbed -type f -mtime +7 -print0 | xargs -0 -P$(nproc) pigz -k.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: Need find files older than 7 days and compress in parallel. Use find with -mtime +7 and -type f, then xargs -P? Use pigz for parallel gzip. Command: find testbed -type f -mtime +7 -print0 | xargs -0 -P$(nproc) pigz -k? But compress in place? pigz -k keeps original. Use -p? Actually pigz -p threads. But parallel: xargs -P. So: find testbed -type f -mtime +7 -print0 | xargs -0 -P$(nproc) pigz -k.

task.query: Compress in parallel regular files in the testbed directory tree that were last modified more than 7 days ago
task.gold: find /testbed -type f  -mtime +7 | tee compressedP.list | xargs -I{} -P10 compress {} &

record.query: Compress in parallel regular files in the testbed directory tree that were last modified more than 7 days ago
record.gold: find /testbed -type f  -mtime +7 | tee compressedP.list | xargs -I{} -P10 compress {} &
